body.title: Igor idle: diagnose why boredom doesn't trigger background goals
body.result: BoredomSource was missing category='boredom_detected' on twm_push; seeded PROC_BOREDOM_FOREMAN habit (twm_trigger=BOREDOM_DETECTED, code_ref=foreman_scan). Loop now: boredom detected → TWM entry → reactive habit → foreman_scan() → queue check → dispatch.
body.description:
**Problem:** Igor gets bored (30 stuck NE cycles) but doesn't do anything about it — doesn't go read, doesn't pick up background goals. The safe_mode watchdog was the wrong fix. The right fix is making the idle-detection → background-goal pathway actually work.

**What to investigate:** How does the NarrativeEngine decide what to do when it has no active thread? What's the mechanism for 'go read something' when bored? Is PROC_READING_BOOTSTRAP seeded? Does the boredom signal reach a habit/goal that spawns reading?

**Affected files:** devices/igor/cognition/ (NE, COA, habits), devices/igor/tools/bootstrap_reader.py
**Design rules:** Igor uses systems (habits, goals, tools) — it doesn't contain them
**Scope boundary:** Diagnose the gap, implement the fix. Do NOT re-introduce safe_mode or any watchdog that blocks capabilities.
**Completion criteria:** When Igor has no active narrative thread, it picks up a background goal (reading or similar) within a reasonable number of NE cycles. Verifiable via log observation.
